case_title: RUTH VANESSA VERDUGO VS. FAITH HARRINGTON

docket_entries.description: REQUEST TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA ORDER FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS GRANTED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68634 (E), CRC 3.52
REQUEST TO APPEAR AT HEARING BY TELEPHONE (SMALL CLAIMS) FILED BY PLAINTIFF VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA
AUTO ACCIDENT CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $10,000.00 FILED BY VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA AS TO DEFENDANT HARRINGTON, FAITH ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR SEP-17-2019 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT 506

DECLARATION OF DUE DILIGENCE IN ATTEMPTING SERVICE ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF & ORDER (SC-100); (FEE FOR SERVICE:$40.00 WAIVER); REMARKS - THIS SERVICE WAS UNSUCCESSFUL BECAUSE DEFENDANT WAS NOT FOUND. ATTEMPTS FOR SERVICE TO NO AVAIL, NO RESPONSE TO NOTES LEFT TO CONTACT THE SHERIFF'S OFFICE. FILED BY PLAINTIFF VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA AS TO DEFENDANT HARRINGTON, FAITH
EXHIBITS FROM EXHIBIT REPORT DATED DEC-02-2019 ORDERED DESTROYED
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF (FEE: $40.00) SERVED AUG-14-2019, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT HARRINGTON, FAITH
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE

SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF SEP-17-2019 - JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF RUTH VANESSA VERDUGO IS PRESENT BY COURTCALL. DEFENDANT FAITH HARRINGTON IS NOT PRESENT. PLAINTIFF EXHIBITS SUBMITTED PRIOR TO TODAYS HEARING ARE VIEWED AND RETAINED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: W. TRUPEK. NOT REPORTED.(506)

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT HARRINGTON, FAITH $10,000.00 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $10,000.00 STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
ORDER RE: REQUEST TO APPEAR AT HEARING BY TELEPHONE [X] DENIED : PLAINTIFF MAY RE APPLY WITH PROPER SERVICE. NOTICE BY FACEBOOK INEFFECTIVE.
REQUEST TO APPEAR AT HEARING BY TELEPHONE FILED BY PLAINTIFF VERDUGO, RUTH VANESSA
ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT, FULLY SATISFIED, AS TO DEFENDANT HARRINGTON, FAITH
